sourceMovie.title: The New Mutants
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The New Mutants (2020) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The New Mutants clicked for you, Resident Evil (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The New Mutants clicked for you, Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The New Mutants?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil, Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Extinction—each shares DNA with The New Mutants while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The New Mutants?
faq.answer: The New Mutants (2020) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The New Mutants part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "X-Anthology" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
